{"key_event":"Industrialization amid Reconstruction retreat","reasoning":"Civil society remained very strong through corporations, labor unions, urban machines, immigrant associations, and a lively press, but gains were offset by repression of radical labor and severe weakening of Black, Chinese, and Native collective power. State power rose modestly as the federal government gained fiscal credibility and new civil-service, immigration, railroad-regulatory, and reservation/allotment capacities, even while it retreated from enforcing Black civil rights and remained weak against corporate power.","society_change":-0.1,"society_power":8.0,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":7.6}
